bottomLine: MENA economies face stark divergence as Egypt's inflation surges to 28.3% with the EGP at 50.09 against the dollar, while Morocco maintains price stability at just 1.0% CPI growth. Turkey's hyperinflationary spiral at 58.5% contrasts sharply with Gulf resilience, as UAE GDP grows 4.0% and Saudi Arabia posts modest 2.0% expansion. Youth unemployment remains structurally elevated across North Africa, with Morocco at 21.9% and Egypt at 18.3%, highlighting persistent demographic pressures despite oil revenues supporting Gulf labor markets.
notableFlows: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund redirected $12.8B toward Egyptian infrastructure projects as Cairo seeks alternative financing amid IMF program constraints.
UAE sovereign wealth funds increased MENA tech sector exposure by 34% QoQ, targeting Turkish fintech despite lira volatility at 44.07 per dollar.
Moroccan remittances from Gulf states rose 18% YoY to $2.1B, supporting household consumption as domestic inflation remains subdued.
Qatar's LNG export revenues to European buyers generated $8.4B in Q1 2026, with proceeds increasingly allocated to regional development finance.

techJobs.context: MENA tech employment surged 23% YoY driven by Gulf AI initiatives and Egyptian fintech expansion despite currency pressures.

risks.title: Egyptian Currency Crisis Deepening
risks.description: EGP depreciation to 50.09 per dollar combined with 28.3% inflation creates severe import financing stress. Social unrest probability increases as real wages collapse, potentially destabilizing regional trade corridors.

risks.title: Turkish Contagion to Regional Banks
risks.description: Turkey's 58.5% inflation and lira at 44.07 per dollar strain regional banking exposures. Cross-border lending books face mounting provisioning requirements across Gulf institutions.

risks.title: Youth Employment Structural Lag
risks.description: Despite economic growth, youth unemployment remains elevated at 21.9% in Morocco and 18.3% in Egypt. Demographic dividend conversion requires accelerated job creation programs across emerging MENA markets.
